rationale: Adds two visible links with the same accesskey value, creating duplicate accesskeys that violate the uniqueness requirement.
Injected an image map with an active <area> lacking alt or ARIA text, which directly violates area-alt.

Injects a div with role="img" and aria-checked, which is not allowed for the img role. aria-checked is not a global attribute and is only permitted on checkbox/radio-like roles, triggering aria-allowed-attr.
Adds a ul element with role="button", which is not an allowed role for ul, triggering the aria-allowed-role violation.
Adds a div with role='button' and an empty aria-label, providing no accessible name, which triggers the aria-command-name rule.
Adds a dialog with an empty aria-label, resulting in no accessible name and triggering the aria-dialog-name rule.

Adds an with role="doc-chapter". DPUB roles require elements with implicit fallback semantics (e.g., sectioning/grouping/heading/aside/body). is embedded content and lacks the required implicit fallback, triggering aria-dpub-role-fallback.
Adds aria-hidden="true" to the document body, directly triggering the aria-hidden-body rule.

Sets aria-hidden=true on the existing cookie banner that contains focusable links; if not present, injects a new aria-hidden container with a focusable anchor to trigger the aria-hidden-focus violation.
Appends a contenteditable div with role='textbox' without aria-label or aria-labelledby, creating an ARIA input field that lacks an accessible name.
Injects a role='meter' element with value attributes but no aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title, ensuring the meter lacks an accessible name and triggers the rule.
Adds a role="progressbar" without aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title, ensuring it lacks an accessible name and triggers aria-progressbar-name.
Injected a widget with role="slider" but omitted required ARIA attributes aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, and aria-valuenow, which triggers the aria-required-attr rule.
Injected a container with role=tablist but without any role=tab descendants, violating the ARIA required children rule.
Injected a standalone element with role="menuitem" outside of a required parent (menu/menubar), triggering aria-required-parent.
Adds aria-roledescription to a generic div with no implicit or explicit role, which violates the rule requiring a valid role when using aria-roledescription.
Adds a visible element with an invalid ARIA role value (role="landmarrk"), which violates the requirement that role values must be valid.
Creates an element with role="text" that contains a focusable descendant (an anchor), which violates the rule.
Adds a focusable ARIA toggle (role="switch") with aria-checked but no accessible name, triggering aria-toggle-field-name.
Adds a role="tooltip" element with an empty aria-label and no inner text, creating a tooltip with no accessible name to trigger aria-tooltip-name.
Inserts a widget container with a role="treeitem" that has no text, aria-label, or aria-labelledby, ensuring it lacks an accessible name and triggers the rule.
Introduces a global ARIA attribute (aria-hidden) with an invalid token value ('pizza'), which violates the allowed values ('true', 'false', or 'undefined') and triggers aria-valid-attr-value.
Adds an element with an invalid ARIA attribute name (aria-visible), which violates the requirement that aria-* attributes use recognized names.
Adds a visible <audio> player without any <track kind="captions">, triggering the audio-caption rule.
Sets an invalid autocomplete value ("emial") on a personal data field, causing the autocomplete-valid rule to fail.
Adds a paragraph with inline letter-spacing set using !important, preventing user/custom stylesheets from adjusting text spacing and triggering avoid-inline-spacing.
Inserted a deprecated <blink> element into the main content so the axe "blink" rule flags its presence.
Adds a focusable <button> with an empty aria-label and no text, resulting in no discernible accessible name and triggering the button-name rule.
Replaces native landmark elements (header, nav, main, footer, aside) to remove landmarks and hides all headings and skip links so no bypass mechanism (heading, skip link, or landmark) remains.
Adds multiple checkboxes sharing the same name without a fieldset/legend or ARIA group, creating a clear checkboxgroup violation.
Inserts two visible links that share the same accesskey value ('g'), creating duplicate accesskeys and triggering the rule.
Adds an image map with clickable <area> elements lacking alt/aria text, triggering the area-alt rule for active areas without accessible names.
Inserts an element with role="tab" that uses aria-checked, an attribute not allowed for that role, which triggers the aria-allowed-attr rule.
Adds a <ul> with role="button", which is not an allowed ARIA role for unordered lists, triggering the aria-allowed-role rule.
Injects a visible element with role=button and an empty aria-label, resulting in no accessible name and triggering aria-command-name.
Adds a visible element with role="dialog" and an empty aria-label, resulting in no accessible name and triggering aria-dialog-name.
Adds a DPUB role (doc-index) to a <table>, which lacks an implicit fallback role per the rule’s allowed element categories, triggering aria-dpub-role-fallback.
Sets aria-hidden="true" on the document body, which directly triggers the aria-hidden-body violation.
Creates a container with aria-hidden=true that contains a focusable anchor, which violates the rule.
Adds a div with role=combobox and no aria-label or aria-labelledby, which lacks an accessible name and triggers aria-input-field-name.
Injects a role="meter" element without aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title, which fails the aria-meter-name rule.
Adds a visible element with role="progressbar" whose aria-labelledby points to a non-existent ID, leaving it without an accessible name and triggering the aria-progressbar-name rule.
Adds a focusable element with role="slider" but omits required aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, and aria-valuenow, triggering aria-required-attr.
Inserts a div with role=list but without required role=listitem children, triggering the aria-required-children rule.
Adds a div with role="option" outside of any role="listbox" parent, violating aria-required-parent.
Adds a div with aria-roledescription but no explicit or implicit role, which violates the rule.
Injects an element with an invalid ARIA role value ('navigtion'), which violates the requirement that role values must be valid and not abstract, triggering the aria-roles rule.
Adds an element with role="text" that contains a focusable link descendant, violating the requirement that role="text" must have no focusable descendants.
Inserts an ARIA toggle (role='switch') without any accessible name (no aria-label/aria-labelledby or text), which triggers the aria-toggle-field-name rule.
Adds a visible element with role="tooltip" that lacks an accessible name (empty content, aria-labelledby to a missing element), which violates the aria-tooltip-name rule.
Appends a role=treeitem with no accessible name by using aria-labelledby referencing a non-existent element, which triggers the aria-treeitem-name rule.
Adds a new element using the global ARIA attribute aria-hidden with an invalid value (rtue), violating the rule that ARIA attributes must have valid values.
Adds an element with unrecognized ARIA attributes (aria-visible and misspelled aria-labledby), which violates the aria-valid-attr rule.
Adds a visible <audio> element that lacks a <track kind="captions"> (it only has a subtitles track), which violates the requirement that each audio element include captions.
Adds an input that uses an invalid autocomplete token (first-name instead of given-name), which violates the autocomplete-valid rule.
Adds visible text with inline line-height, letter-spacing, and word-spacing declared with !important, preventing custom stylesheet adjustments and triggering avoid-inline-spacing.
Injects a deprecated <blink> element into the DOM, which directly violates the rule that disallows use of <blink>.
Inserts a focusable <button> with no discernible text by using aria-labelledby that points to a non-existent element, triggering the button-name rule.
Removes internal skip links and hides both landmarks and headings from the accessibility tree, leaving no bypass mechanism for repeated blocks.
Added multiple checkboxes sharing the same name without a fieldset/legend or ARIA group/aria-labelledby, which violates the checkboxgroup rule.
Injects two interactive elements that share the same accesskey value, violating the uniqueness requirement.
Adds an image map with clickable <area> elements that lack alt or ARIA labels, triggering the area-alt rule.
Injects a div with role="img" using aria-checked, which is not an allowed attribute for the img role, triggering aria-allowed-attr.
Assigning role="heading" to a button is not permitted by ARIA in HTML, triggering the aria-allowed-role violation.
Appends an element with role="button" that lacks any accessible name (no text, aria-label, or aria-labelledby), which violates the aria-command-name rule.
Adds a role="dialog" with aria-labelledby pointing to a nonexistent element and no aria-label or title, resulting in no accessible name.
Adds a DPUB ARIA role (doc-chapter) to a div, which lacks an implicit fallback role, triggering aria-dpub-role-fallback.
Adds aria-hidden="true" to the document body, which makes all content inaccessible to assistive technologies and directly triggers the rule.
Injects a container with aria-hidden="true" that contains a focusable button, directly violating the rule.
Adds a div with role="textbox" whose aria-labelledby references a non-existent element, leaving it without an accessible name and triggering the rule.
Adds a visible element with role="meter" and an empty aria-label, resulting in no accessible name and triggering the aria-meter-name violation.
Adds a role=progressbar element with an empty aria-label, resulting in no accessible name and triggering aria-progressbar-name.
Inserts a div with role="slider" but without aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, or aria-valuenow, which are required for sliders.
Injected a container with role="menu" but provided only link/button children instead of required roles like menuitem/menuitemcheckbox/menuitemradio, violating aria-required-children.
Adds a role="menuitem" directly under body without a parent with role="menu" or "menubar", violating the required parent relationship.
Adds aria-roledescription to a paragraph with no implicit or explicit ARIA role, which violates the rule.
Inserts an element with an invalid ARIA role value ('buton'), which violates the requirement that roles must be valid.
Creates an element with role="text" that contains a focusable descendant (a link with tabindex=0), which violates the rule.
Adds an ARIA toggle (role=checkbox) with no accessible name by referencing a non-existent aria-labelledby target.
Adds a role="tooltip" element with an empty aria-label, resulting in no accessible name and triggering the rule.
Appends a tree with a treeitem that has an empty aria-label and no text, resulting in no accessible name.
Adds a div with aria-hidden set to an invalid token (rtue), violating the requirement that ARIA attribute values must be valid.
Adds an element with a non-existent ARIA attribute (aria-visible), causing the aria-valid-attr rule to fail.
Inserts an <audio> element that lacks a <track> with kind="captions" (only a subtitles track), directly violating the audio-caption rule.
Adds an input with an invalid autocomplete token list (unsupported token and missing a valid field name), which violates autocomplete-valid.
Adds a paragraph with inline spacing properties marked !important, which prevents user stylesheets from adjusting text spacing and triggers the rule.
Inserts a deprecated <blink> element into the DOM, which directly triggers the axe "blink" rule that flags any presence of blink tags.
Appends a <button> element with no discernible text or accessible name attributes, triggering the button-name rule.

Removes headings, skip links, and landmark regions, then adds a large repeated block of links without a main landmark. With no skip link, heading, or landmark present, the page lacks a way to bypass repeated blocks, triggering the bypass rule.
Adds multiple checkboxes sharing the same name without a fieldset/legend or ARIA group, triggering the checkboxgroup rule.
Adds two interactive elements (a link and a button) that share the same accesskey value 'g', creating duplicate accesskey assignments that violate the uniqueness requirement.
Adds a client-side image map where active <area> elements lack accessible names (no alt/aria) to trigger area-alt.
Adds a div with role="img" using aria-checked, which is not permitted for the img role, triggering aria-allowed-attr.
Assigns role="button" to a <ul>, which is not an allowed role for that element, triggering aria-allowed-role.
Injects a focusable element with role=button and an empty aria-label, resulting in no accessible name and triggering aria-command-name.
Adds a role="dialog" element without aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title, causing it to lack an accessible name and trigger aria-dialog-name.
Adds DPUB roles (doc-abstract, doc-toc, doc-glossary) to generic elements (div/span) without implicit fallback roles, triggering the rule.
Sets aria-hidden="true" on the document body, which directly violates the rule that forbids hiding the entire body from assistive technologies.
Sets aria-hidden=true on the existing footer which contains many focusable links/buttons, creating focusable descendants inside an aria-hidden element.

Inserts a div with role="textbox" that lacks aria-label or aria-labelledby. Wrapping it in a label provides only visual text, not a programmatic name for ARIA widgets, triggering aria-input-field-name.
Adds a role="meter" element without aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title, so it lacks an accessible name and triggers aria-meter-name.
Appends a role=progressbar without aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title, ensuring it lacks an accessible name and triggers the rule.
Adds a checkbox role without the required aria-checked attribute, triggering the aria-required-attr rule.
Adds a role='menu' element without any required children (menuitem/menuitemcheckbox/menuitemradio), triggering aria-required-children.
Adds a standalone element with role="tab" that is not contained within a role="tablist", violating the required parent relationship.
Adds aria-roledescription to a generic div without an implicit or explicit role, which violates the rule requiring aria-roledescription only on elements with a semantic role.
Appends a visible element with an invalid ARIA role value (notarole), which violates the rule requiring valid role tokens.
Inserts a span with role="text" that contains a focusable anchor link, violating the requirement that role="text" elements must not have focusable descendants.
Adds an ARIA toggle control (role='switch') without any accessible name (no aria-label/aria-labelledby/text), which triggers aria-toggle-field-name.
Injects a tooltip element with an empty aria-label, resulting in no accessible name and triggering the aria-tooltip-name violation.
Inserts a tree with a treeitem that has no text, aria-label, or aria-labelledby, ensuring the treeitem lacks an accessible name and triggers the rule.
Adds a div with aria-hidden set to an invalid token (rtue), violating the required true/false/undefined values.
Adds an invalid ARIA attribute (aria-visible) to an existing element, violating the requirement that ARIA attributes must have valid names.
Adds an <audio> element without any <track kind="captions">, triggering the audio-caption rule.
Adds an input with an invalid autocomplete value ('first-name'), which violates the required token set and triggers autocomplete-valid.
Adds an inline line-height with !important to a text element, preventing user styles from adjusting spacing and triggering avoid-inline-spacing.
Injects a deprecated <blink> element into the page, ensuring the rule flags its presence.
Appends a focusable <button> with no discernible text and no aria naming; the value attribute does not provide an accessible name, triggering the button-name rule.
Removes all landmarks, internal skip links, and headings so there is no mechanism to bypass repeated blocks, triggering the bypass rule.
Injects three checkboxes that share the same name without a fieldset/legend, role="group", or shared aria-labelledby, triggering the checkboxgroup rule.
Adds two interactive links sharing the same accesskey value, creating duplicate access keys to trigger the accesskeys rule.
Adds an image map with a clickable <area> missing alt text, which violates the area-alt rule.
Creates a div with role="button" using aria-checked, which is not allowed for that role, triggering aria-allowed-attr.
Added a ul element with role=button, which is not an allowed role for ul, to trigger aria-allowed-role.
Inserts a div with role="button" and no accessible name (no text, no aria-label/labelledby), which violates aria-command-name.
Inserts a div with role="dialog" and aria-labelledby referencing a non-existent id, creating a dialog without an accessible name to trigger aria-dialog-name.
Adds a DPUB role (doc-footnote) to a table element, which lacks the required implicit fallback role, triggering aria-dpub-role-fallback.
Set aria-hidden="true" on the body so all content is hidden from assistive technologies, directly violating the rule.
Adds a focusable link inside an aria-hidden=true container, which violates the rule that aria-hidden elements must not contain focusable elements.
Adds an ARIA input element (role="textbox") without aria-label or aria-labelledby, ensuring it lacks an accessible name and triggers the rule.
Adds a role='meter' element with no aria-label, aria-labelledby, title, or text content, so it lacks an accessible name and triggers aria-meter-name.
Inserted a div with role="progressbar" that lacks aria-label, aria-labelledby, and title, ensuring it has no accessible name and triggers aria-progressbar-name.
Adds a div with role="checkbox" but without the required aria-checked attribute, which violates the aria-required-attr rule.
Creates a parent with role="tablist" but no children with role="tab", violating required child roles and triggering aria-required-children.
Adds an element with role=menuitem that is not contained within a role=menu or role=menubar, violating aria-required-parent.
Adds aria-roledescription to elements without implicit or explicit roles (p and div), which violates the rule.
Injects a visible element with an invalid ARIA role value ('buton') to trigger the aria-roles rule.
Adds an element with role="text" that contains a focusable link, violating the rule that role="text" must not have focusable descendants.
Adds a role='switch' toggle with no aria-label, aria-labelledby, or text, ensuring it lacks an accessible name.
Adds a visible element with role="tooltip" but with an empty aria-label and a non-existent aria-labelledby target, leaving it without an accessible name to trigger the rule.
Adds ARIA treeitem elements without accessible names (empty content, empty aria-label, or broken aria-labelledby), which triggers the aria-treeitem-name violation.
Adds a visible element with aria-hidden set to an invalid token value ('pizza'), violating ARIA valid attribute value requirements.
Injects an element with an invalid ARIA attribute (aria-visible), which is not a recognized WAI-ARIA attribute name, causing a failure for aria-valid-attr.
Adds an <audio> element with a <track> of kind="subtitles" and no captions track, ensuring it fails the audio-caption rule.
Adds an input with an invalid autocomplete value ('nope'), which violates the required token set for the autocomplete attribute and triggers the autocomplete-valid rule.
Adds a text paragraph with inline style line-height set using !important so user styles cannot adjust spacing, triggering avoid-inline-spacing.
Adds a deprecated <blink> element to the DOM, which directly triggers the axe-core blink rule.
Adds a focusable <button> with no text, aria-label, or aria-labelledby (only an aria-hidden icon), creating a button with no discernible name.
Removes the page’s skip link, converts all headings to non-heading divs, and strips landmark semantics, leaving no bypass mechanism and triggering the bypass rule.
Adds multiple checkbox inputs sharing the same name without grouping them in a fieldset or ARIA group, triggering the checkboxgroup rule.
Adds the same accesskey value to multiple links, creating duplicate accesskey values and violating uniqueness.
Adds an image map with clickable <area> elements lacking alt or aria labels, triggering the area-alt rule.
Adds aria-checked to a link (implicit role=link), which does not permit aria-checked, triggering aria-allowed-attr.
Adds a <ul> with role="button", which is not an allowed role for ul per ARIA in HTML, triggering aria-allowed-role.
Inserts a div with role=button and no text, aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title, creating a command element without an accessible name.
Injects an element with role=alertdialog and an empty aria-label, ensuring it lacks an accessible name and triggers the rule.
Adds a DPUB role (doc-chapter) to a div, which has no implicit ARIA role, causing a missing fallback and triggering the rule.
Adds aria-hidden="true" to the document body, which directly triggers the aria-hidden-body rule.
Adds a container with aria-hidden="true" that contains a focusable button, violating the rule.
Adds a div with role="textbox" lacking an accessible name; wrapping it in a <label> does not name non-labellable elements, triggering aria-input-field-name.
Appends a role="meter" element without aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title, ensuring it lacks an accessible name and triggers the rule.
Inserts a div with role="progressbar" and an empty aria-label, ensuring it has no accessible name and triggers aria-progressbar-name.
Inserts a visible element with role="checkbox" but omits the required aria-checked attribute, directly triggering aria-required-attr.
Adds a role="tablist" without any role="tab" children, violating required owned elements.
Adds a role="menuitem" without a required parent role (menu or menubar), triggering aria-required-parent.
Adds aria-roledescription to a div that has no implicit or explicit ARIA role, violating the rule.
Set an invalid ARIA role value ('notarole') on the main container to violate the rule requiring valid role values.
Insert an element with role="text" that contains a focusable link descendant, which violates the rule requiring no focusable descendants.
Adds an ARIA toggle (role='switch') without an accessible name (aria-labelledby points to a non-existent id), triggering aria-toggle-field-name.
Adds a tooltip role element with an empty aria-label and no text, resulting in no accessible name.
Appends a role=treeitem with no text, aria-label, or aria-labelledby, ensuring it lacks an accessible name and triggers the rule.
Adds a div with aria-hidden set to an invalid value ('pizza'), which violates the allowed true/false tokens.
Sets an invalid ARIA attribute (aria-visible) on the body element, which violates the requirement that ARIA attribute names must be valid.
Injects an audio element without a captions track, triggering the audio-caption violation.
Sets an invalid multi-token autocomplete value ('given-name email') on an input, which is not permitted and triggers the autocomplete-valid rule.
Injects a paragraph with inline line-height set using !important, blocking user overrides and triggering the avoid-inline-spacing rule.
Adds a deprecated <blink> element to the DOM, which is explicitly flagged by the blink rule.
Appends a focusable button with an empty aria-label, resulting in no discernible accessible name and triggering the button-name rule.
Removes all headings, in-page skip links, and landmark regions so the page lacks any mechanism to bypass repeated blocks, triggering the bypass rule.
Adds multiple checkboxes sharing the same name without a fieldset/legend or ARIA group, violating the checkboxgroup rule.
Assigns the same accesskey value to multiple interactive elements, creating duplicate accesskeys that violate uniqueness.
Adds active <area> elements (with href) to an image map without alt or accessible names, triggering the area-alt rule.
Adds a div with role="button" that uses aria-level and aria-checked, which are not allowed for the button role, triggering the aria-allowed-attr rule.
Injected a UL with role=button, which is not an allowed role for UL per ARIA in HTML, triggering aria-allowed-role.
Adds elements with role="button" and role="link" that have no accessible names (empty aria-label or no label), directly triggering the aria-command-name rule.
Adds a dialog with aria-labelledby pointing to a non-existent element, providing no aria-label or title, so the dialog has no accessible name and violates aria-dialog-name.
Adds a div with role="doc-bibliography"; div has no implicit fallback role (e.g., list), triggering the DPUB fallback rule.
Adds aria-hidden="true" to the document body, hiding all content from assistive technologies and triggering the aria-hidden-body rule.
Adds a container with aria-hidden="true" that contains a focusable button and link, which violates the rule.

Adds a role="meter" element with required value attributes but no accessible name (no aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title), which triggers the aria-meter-name rule.

Injected a container with role="list" whose children lack role="listitem", violating required ARIA child roles.
Two elements use role="menuitem" without a required parent role (menu or menubar), triggering aria-required-parent.
Adds aria-roledescription to generic elements (div, p) that lack an implicit or explicit role, which violates the rule requiring aria-roledescription only on elements with a supported role.

Prepends a visible button with an invalid ARIA attribute (aria-visible), which violates the rule requiring valid aria-* attribute names.

Removes the skip link, main landmark, and all heading elements so no mechanism remains to bypass repeated blocks, triggering the bypass rule.
Injected multiple checkboxes that share the same name without a fieldset or ARIA group, creating an ungrouped checkbox set that triggers the checkboxgroup rule.
Assigns the same accesskey value to two elements (header and footer logo links or injected links), creating duplicate accesskey values and triggering the accesskeys rule.

Creates a container with role="tablist" but no descendants with role="tab", violating the required-children condition.

Appends an <audio> element without any <track kind="captions">, which violates the audio-caption rule.

Removes all bypass mechanisms by stripping the main and other landmark regions, replacing all headings with generic elements, and removing any in-page skip links so the page lacks headings, skip links, and landmarks, triggering the bypass rule.
Adds multiple checkboxes sharing the same name without a fieldset/legend or ARIA group, triggering the checkboxgroup rule.
